name: Apple MacBook Pro 14-inch (M4 Pro, 2025)
introText: The MacBook Pro 14 with M4 Pro delivers sustained professional performance with a Mini LED XDR display, active cooling, and 24-hour battery life — the best laptop available for demanding creative workflows at $2,199 CAD.
whatWorks: M4 Pro chip with active cooling sustains peak performance indefinitely — unlike the fanless Air, the Pro handles hours-long video rendering, large ML model training, and 3D work without throttling.

Liquid Retina XDR display with 1600 nits peak HDR is the best laptop screen available — Mini LED with local dimming produces true blacks and blazing highlights that no OLED competitor matches for contrast.
worthKnowing: Most users will never need M4 Pro performance — the base M4 Air handles 95% of creative tasks at $900 less. The Pro makes sense for professional video editors, developers compiling large codebases, and ML practitioners.
HDMI and SD card slot included — unlike the Air, the Pro has full port selection built in, reducing hub dependency.

commonComplaints.title: Price premium hard to justify for casual use
commonComplaints.body: At $900 more than the Air, the MacBook Pro is overkill for email, browsing, and document work. Most users are better served by the Air.

commonComplaints.title: Notch in display divides opinion
commonComplaints.body: The camera notch at the top of the display is visible in windowed apps. Most users adapt quickly but it remains a design criticism.

commonComplaints.title: Base 512GB still fills for video pros
commonComplaints.body: Professional video editors working with RAW footage fill 512GB in weeks. The 1TB upgrade ($200+ more) is recommended for media production.

verdict: The MacBook Pro 14 M4 Pro is the best laptop for professionals who do sustained heavy compute — video editors, developers, ML practitioners, and 3D artists. If you're not in those categories, the MacBook Air delivers 90% of the experience at 60% of the price.

realBuyers.quote: Professional colorist. Replaced a Mac Pro tower. DaVinci Resolve performance is staggering — renders 4K ProRes in real time.
24-hour battery is real. I've gone three days without charging while traveling.
Best laptop I've ever owned. The XDR display alone is worth the premium for photo editing.

sources.title: MacBook Pro M4 Review — The Verge

metaDescription: MacBook Pro 14 M4 Pro review Canada: 24GB RAM, 512GB SSD, XDR display, 24-hr battery. $2,199 CAD. Best pro laptop for creative professionals in Canada.
